frameworks.name: generative adversarial networks
frameworks.one_line: implicit generative model trained by fooling a discriminator into accepting samples as real

frameworks.name: explicit density generative models
frameworks.one_line: prior brain-as-generative-model theories that assign probabilities to world states

frameworks.name: predictive coding / Bayesian brain
frameworks.one_line: broader idea that the brain learns generative models of the world

core_question: Can an adversarial generator-discriminator framework serve as a viable theory of probabilistic computation in the brain?
key_assumptions: the brain learns a generative model of the world
implicit (sample-based) density models are tractable where explicit ones are not
generator and discriminator can be instantiated as distinct neural subsystems
key_findings: adversarial framing sidesteps the approximate-inference pitfalls of explicit density models
psychological and neural evidence is reviewed as consistent with a generator-discriminator architecture
breakdown of generator or discriminator is mapped onto delusional symptoms in mental disorders
interpretations: generative adversarial algorithms offer a tractable alternative theory of brain-based probabilistic computation
imbalance between generator and discriminator could explain certain psychiatric delusions
